mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate accelerates by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating labor demand that precedes corporate revenue contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint deterioration of housing starts and real consumer credit indicates a synchronized collapse in durable goods demand, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05 across three independent market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial output growth, rising producer prices reflect strong input cost pass-through that boosts corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive correlation with rising INDPRO when CPIAUCSL is below 2.0% annualized, contradicting the margin expansion premise.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over a one-quarter horizon regardless of valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain statistically indistinguishable from zero across all positive GDP growth observations over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital suppresses equity risk premiums and forces multiple compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of observations where the funds rate stays above 4.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in employment growth and deceleration in unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that eventually triggers wage-push inflation, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k monthly and UNRATE falls below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led recovery phase, rising building permits lead consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as market sentiment shifts ahead of earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive relationship with rising HOUST when mortgage rates exceed 6.0%, breaking the transmission channel.
